rows:
In Physics, Cryogenics is the study of the production and behavior of materials
at extremely low temperatures (below −292.00°F).

An event that disrupts normal operating conditions and can pose a threat to
human life, health, property, or the environment, if not immediately controlled.

Substance made of hydrogen and carbon (among other elements).
Common hydrocarbons found in Natural Gas (AKA “Fuel Gas” @ Cove Point
LNG):
• Methane
• Ethane
• Propane
• Butane
• Pentane

Liquefied Natural Gas: Natural gas composed primarily of methane that is
cooled to a temperature of about −260°F at atmospheric pressure where it
condenses to a liquid.

Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), called simply “propane” or “butane,” are
flammable mixtures of hydrocarbon gases stored in pressurized containers.
Material Safety Data Sheet / Safety Data Sheet
key_facts: CFM: DEFINITION = Cubic Feet per Minute

Cryogenic: DEFINITION = In Physics, Cryogenics is the study of the production and behavior of materials
at extremely low temperatures (below −292.00°F).

Emergency: DEFINITION = An event that disrupts normal operating conditions and can pose a threat to
human life, health, property, or the environment, if not immediately controlled.

Hydrocarbons: DEFINITION = Substance made of hydrogen and carbon (among other elements).
Common hydrocarbons found in Natural Gas (AKA “Fuel Gas” @ Cove Point
LNG):
• Methane
• Ethane
• Propane
• Butane
• Pentane

LNG: DEFINITION = Liquefied Natural Gas: Natural gas composed primarily of methane that is
cooled to a temperature of about −260°F at atmospheric pressure where it
condenses to a liquid.

LPG: DEFINITION = Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), called simply “propane” or “butane,” are
flammable mixtures of hydrocarbon gases stored in pressurized containers.
MSDS/SDS: DEFINITION = Material Safety Data Sheet / Safety Data Sheet
psi: DEFINITION = Pounds per Square Inch
psig: DEFINITION = Pounds per Square Inch (Gauge)
